articles.title: IT
articles.summary: AI-driven attacks are getting bigger, faster, and more sophisticated. But Vanta's newest State of Trust [4] survey of 3,500 leaders shows that businesses are spending way more time and energy proving trust than building it:

articles.title: Get your copy: _The State of Trust Report_
NVIDIA PROFITS SOAR, SOOTHING INVESTOR JITTERS OVER AI BOOM
articles.summary: Nvidia reported record sales at its quarterly earnings report yesterday. The company hit a record $57 billion in the October quarter, up 62% from the year-earlier quarter. Nvidia estimates sales for the current quarter will reach $65 billion. Shares in the company rose up to 6.5% in after-hours trading.

articles.title: TESTING SHOWS APPLE N1 WI-FI CHIP IMPROVES ON OLDER BROADCOM CHIPS IN EVERY WAY
articles.summary: The Apple N1 is Apple's first in-house chip designed to handle local wireless connections. It supports Wi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 6, and the Thread smart home connection protocol. While the N1 isn't the best chip on the market, it delivers higher download and upload speeds on Wi-Fi compared to the iPhone 16.

articles.title: AGING AS A DISEASE: THE RISE OF LONGEVITY SCIENCE
articles.summary: The frontier of medicine is shifting from fighting disease to pursuing more life. Speakers at the recent Progress Conference 2025 held at Lighthaven spoke about extending life by addressing the biological hallmarks of aging itself: genomic instability, telomere shortening, mitochondrial dysfunction, and cellular senescence. The new frontier is to find targeted interventions that impact these factors. The pursuit of longevity, not survival, is still in its early days. Research is happening largely along non-traditional paths and with non-traditional funding and support models.

articles.title: NEW NANOPARTICLE MRNA VACCINE MAY BE CHEAPER AND 100 TIMES MORE POWERFUL
articles.summary: Researchers at MIT have developed a new lipid nanoparticle that enhances the effectiveness of an mRNA vaccine by a hundred times compared to stand-alone effectiveness in mice. The system could be used to develop vaccines for a number of diseases, including COVID-19 and influenza. By increasing potency and reducing cost per dosage, the system could become an inexpensive way to combat many infectious diseases. It could also help improve the efficacy of some vaccines.

articles.title: DEEP DIVE: ARCHITECTING FOR HIGH-THROUGHPUT INFERENCE WITH GOOGLE'S IRONWOOD TPUS
articles.summary: Traditional hardware struggles to balance the latency and cost demands of AI inference. Google's Ironwood TPUs are purpose-built to handle these INFERENCE-HEAVY WORKLOADS more efficiently than ever before. Join this technical session [9] to unpack the architecture, software stack compatibility, and performance benchmarks for serving next-gen models.

articles.title: BUILDING MORE WITH GPT-5.1-CODEX-MAX
articles.summary: GPT‑5.1-Codex-Max is a new frontier agentic coding model from OpenAI. It is much faster, more intelligent, and more token-efficient at every stage of the development cycle. The model is built for long-running, detailed work. It is available in Codex today. API access is coming soon.

articles.title: PYREFLY
articles.summary: Pyrefly is a type checker and language server for Python that provides lightning-fast type checking and IDE features. It can infer types in most locations and understand a program's control flow to refine static types. Pyrefly aims for large-scale incrementality at the module level. It is under active development with known issues.

articles.title: COMPANIES ARE WARMING UP TO SAYING AI IS THE REASON FOR JOB CUTS
articles.summary: AI was referenced in roughly a fifth of total job cuts announced in the US last month. Executives have been touting efficiency gains from AI as a reason for a shrinking or flat workforce. Even when companies aren't saying AI helps eliminate jobs, they are mentioning it as a reason to raise the bar on new hires. There is still some uncertainty about how useful AI tools are and how to fit them into daily work - if tech firms can ease that uncertainty, it may open the door to wider corporate AI adoption.

articles.title: THE FTC'S META ANTITRUST IMPLOSION
articles.summary: Meta worked to make Instagram and WhatsApp successful by improving their design and increasing their revenue. The company has been forced to evolve in response to competition, increasing smartphone usage, and a massive leap in AI-powered algorithms. To prove that Meta holds a monopoly would require proving that TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, and X aren't real competitors. Meta's enormous profits aren't evidence of monopoly power.

articles.title: SHIP DOMAIN INTEGRATIONS IN HOURS WITH THE NAME.COM API
articles.summary: Use the API that powers Vercel, Replit, and Netlify's domain services. Built for AI agents and developers with OpenAPI spec and MCP support—integrate search, registration, and management. Start

articles.title: EPIC GAMES ANNOUNCES SWEEPING CROSS-PLATFORM COLLAB' BRINGING UNITY'S GAMES AND COMMERCE PLATFORM TO FORTNITE
articles.summary: The collaboration will also see Unity bring Unreal Engine support to its platform.

articles.title: NVIDIA SAYS THERE'S ‘NO ASSURANCE' OF FINAL AGREEMENT WITH OPENAI DESPITE $100 BILLION PACT
articles.summary: Despite the uncertainty of the agreement, Nvidia executives continue to sound bullish on the company's work with OpenAI.

articles.title: MOJO-V
articles.summary: Mojo-V is a new RISC-V extension that implements secret computation, enabling secure, efficient, and data-oblivious execution.

articles.title: NETFLIX'S WARNER BROS. BID WOULD INCLUDE THEATER RELEASES
articles.summary: Netflix will continue releasing Warner Bros. films in theaters if it is successful in buying the company.

articles.title: BITTER ECONOMICS
articles.summary: AGI will reduce a massively broad class of technical problems to a simple matter of economics.

articles.title: DELIVERY APP TIPPING ISN'T ABOUT FEEDBACK MECHANISMS
articles.summary: Tipping is a tool that delivery companies use to maximize the price paid by consumers.
